prompt: A MeridianCare Clinic Operations platform capable of running entirely on an internal workstation or clinic LAN provides end-to-end patient intake, triage, and appointment execution without relying on any external services. Patients and front-desk staff use an English-language HTMX-driven web interface for fast, form-based workflows: creating an account with a username and password (no SMS/email codes), maintaining basic demographics, and completing pre-visit health assessments that include common scales (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) plus clinic-defined indicators such as blood pressure category, fall-risk flags, and medication adherence. The assessment screen shows immediate, explainable risk stratification (e.g., Low/Moderate/High) with the specific rules and contributing answers displayed, then generates a traceable assessment record tied to the visit. Scheduling supports clinician and room/resource calendars with slot capacity (default 1 patient per 15-minute slot per clinician), conflict detection, holiday closures, bulk template scheduling, and a reservation hold that expires after 10 minutes if the patient abandons checkout. Visits move through a guided state machine visible on a shared dashboard—Booked, Pending Payment, Checked In, Seen, Canceled, or No-Show—with clear UI feedback for locked states, duplicate-click prevention, and timestamped milestones for operational reporting. For clinics that offer in-house supply drop-off or mobile services, administrators configure service coverage zones by neighborhood/ZIP grouping and distance bands from the clinic, setting per-zone minimum order thresholds (e.g., $25.00), flat delivery fees (e.g., $5.00), and allowable delivery windows (e.g., 9:00 AM–12:00 PM, 1:00 PM–5:00 PM) to avoid overpromising outside coverage. The backend uses Flask to implement REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial updates, enforcing idempotent transitions with request tokens, server-side validation, and auditable change logs that record who changed what, when, and why. SQLite provides offline persistence for accounts, roles (Administrator, Clinician, Front Desk, Patient), visit orders and state transitions, assessment definitions and versions, assessment results with explanation snapshots, scheduling resources and holds, zone configurations, and operational telemetry. Automated periodic reassessments and reminders run via a local scheduler within the app process, defaulting to every 90 days for chronic-care templates and 24 hours before a booked visit, with reminders shown in-app at login rather than sent externally. Security and privacy controls assume a closed network but still require HTTPS (self-signed certificates allowed), CSRF protection, strict input validation to prevent SQL injection and XSS, signed request timestamps with a 5-minute anti-replay window for sensitive actions, and rate limiting (e.g., 10 login attempts per 10 minutes per account plus IP). Sensitive fields are classified and protected with masking in the UI (e.g., showing only last 4 of an ID), encryption at rest for stored tokens and clinical notes, and encryption in transit. Users can export their personal data locally as a file and request deletion; deletions anonymize clinical records while preserving legally required audit trails. Observability is fully local: structured logs, slow-query reporting, request correlation IDs for traceability, lightweight health checks, and anomaly alerts (e.g., repeated failed logins, unusual new-device sessions) displayed on an admin operations screen.
